name: Kawashima v. Holder
question: Is filing a false statement on a corporate tax return an aggravated felony involving fraud or deceit, which would render the immigrant removable?
description: A case in which the Court held that filing a false tax return in violation of 26 U.S.C. Section 7206 qualifies as an aggravated felony under the Immigration and Nationality Act if the Government's revenue loss exceeds $10,000.
